q.en: After you flush a toilet, where does the dirty water from homes end up?
q.zh: 冲马桶后，家里的脏水最终去了哪里？

options.en: It flows into the local sewerage system, which carries it to a treatment plant.
options.zh: 流入当地的污水管网系统，被输送到污水处理厂。

options.en: It goes straight into the local drinking water. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 直接流入当地的饮用水中。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: It evaporates into the air on its own.
options.zh: 自己蒸发到空气中。

options.en: It is dumped straight into rivers by trucks.
options.zh: 被卡车直接倒进河里。

explain.en: The book says: 'all of this dirty water ends up in the local sewerage system' and 'Sewer pipes carry waste to the sewage treatment plant.'
explain.zh: 书里说：“所有这些脏水最终都进入了当地的污水管网系统”，而且“污水管道把废弃物输送到污水处理厂”。

q.zh: 在这本书中，“sewage（污水）”指的是什么？

options.en: A type of plant that cleans water.
options.zh: 一种能净化水的植物。

options.en: Clean rainwater collected from storms. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 从暴风雨中收集的干净雨水。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The gas produced by a digester.
options.zh: 消化池产生的气体。

options.en: Human waste that is carried away from buildings through pipes.
options.zh: 通过管道从建筑物中输送走的人类废弃物。

explain.en: The glossary defines sewage as 'human waste that is carried away from buildings through a system of pipes.'
explain.zh: 词汇表把 sewage 定义为“通过管道系统从建筑物中输送走的人类废弃物”。

q.en: In a common sewage treatment process, what is the FIRST step?
q.zh: 在常见的污水处理过程中，第一步是什么？

options.en: A big screen removes large items such as rocks, sticks, and garbage.
options.zh: 一个大筛网先去除石头、树枝和垃圾等大件物品。

options.en: The water is sent directly to farms.
options.zh: 水被直接送到农场。

options.en: The solid waste is sent to a digester. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 固体废弃物被送进消化池。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Bacteria are pumped out of the water.
options.zh: 把细菌从水里抽出来。

explain.en: STEP 1 in the book: 'A big screen (think of a giant kitchen strainer) removes large items such as rocks, sticks, and garbage.'
explain.zh: 书中的第一步：“一个大筛网（想象一个巨大的厨房漏勺）去除石头、树枝和垃圾等大件物品。”

q.en: What does the word 'digester' mean in this book?
q.zh: 在这本书中，“digester（消化池）”是什么意思？

options.en: A filter that removes salt from water. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 去除水中盐分的过滤器。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A type of storm sewer pipe.
options.zh: 一种暴雨下水管道。

options.en: A giant tank where microorganisms break down solid waste.
options.zh: 一个巨大的罐子，微生物在其中分解固体废弃物。

options.en: A machine that measures water levels.
options.zh: 测量水位的机器。

explain.en: The glossary says a digester is 'a large tank in a sewage treatment plant where microorganisms break down solid waste.'
explain.zh: 词汇表说消化池是“污水处理厂里一个巨大的罐子，微生物在其中分解固体废弃物”。

q.en: How is a septic system different from a city sewerage system?
q.zh: 化粪池系统与城市污水管网系统有什么不同？

options.en: A septic system sends waste to a large city treatment plant.
options.zh: 化粪池系统把废弃物送到大型城市处理厂。

options.en: A septic system serves only one home and treats waste right on the property.
options.zh: 化粪池系统只服务一个家庭，并在房屋所在地就地处理废弃物。

options.en: A septic system is only used in big cities.
options.zh: 化粪池系统只在大城市使用。

options.en: A septic system uses screens and filters exactly like a city plant. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 化粪池系统使用和城市处理厂完全一样的筛网和过滤器。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says a septic system 'is a small sewage treatment system that serves only one home' made of a septic tank and drainage field.
explain.zh: 书里说化粪池系统“是一个只服务一个家庭的小型污水处理系统”，由化粪池和排水场组成。

q.en: Why do treatment plants add helpful bacteria to the sludge?
q.zh: 污水处理厂为什么要往污泥里加入有益的细菌？

options.en: The bacteria eat the sludge and help break it down.
options.zh: 细菌吃掉污泥并帮助分解它。

options.en: To make the water taste better.
options.zh: 让水更好喝。

options.en: To kill all the fish in the water.
options.zh: 杀死水里所有的鱼。

options.en: To fill the digester with gas only. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 仅仅为了让消化池充满气体。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book explains: 'These helpful bacteria eat the sludge to break it down.'
explain.zh: 书中解释道：“这些有益的细菌吃掉污泥，把它分解掉。”

q.en: According to the book, what happens to about half of the sludge in the digester?
q.zh: 根据这本书，消化池里大约一半的污泥会怎样？

options.en: It is sent to the moon.
options.zh: 被送到月球上。

options.en: About half turns into gas used to help power the plant's machines; the rest is dried and used on farm fields.
options.zh: 大约一半变成气体，用来帮助驱动处理厂的机器；其余被晒干后用于农田。

options.en: It turns into drinking water.
options.zh: 变成饮用水。

options.en: It is flushed back into people's homes. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book as mentioned in a later chapter
options.zh: 被冲回人们的家里。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）（正如后文某一章提到的）

explain.en: The book states: 'About half of the sludge turns into gas, some of which the treatment plant uses to help power its machines. The other half ... is dried. It's often used on farm fields.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：“大约一半的污泥变成气体，处理厂用其中部分气体来帮助驱动机器。另一半……被晒干，常施用于农田。”

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To persuade people to stop using toilets.
options.zh: 说服人们不要再使用马桶。

options.en: To describe how to build a house.
options.zh: 描述如何建造一所房子。

options.en: To explain where sewage goes and how it is treated.
options.zh: 解释污水去了哪里以及如何被处理。

options.en: To entertain readers with a scary story about monsters.
options.zh: 用一个关于怪物的恐怖故事娱乐读者。

explain.en: The Focus Question is 'Where does sewage go, and how is it treated?' The whole book answers that question.
explain.zh: 书的焦点问题是“污水去了哪里，又是如何被处理的？”，整本书都在回答这个问题。

q.en: How does the author seem to feel about modern sewerage systems?
q.zh: 作者对待现代污水管网系统的态度是怎样的？

options.en: The author wants them replaced by outhouses.
options.zh: 作者希望用户外厕所取代它们。

options.en: The author believes they cause deadly diseases. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 作者认为它们会引发致命疾病。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The author thinks they are gross and useless.
options.zh: 作者认为它们又恶心又没用。

options.en: The author appreciates them and says we should thank our modern sewerage system.
options.zh: 作者很感激它们，并说我们应该感谢现代污水管网系统。

explain.en: The book ends: 'So, the next time you flush, take a moment to thank your modern sewerage system!'
explain.zh: 书的结尾说：“所以，下次你冲马桶时，花点时间感谢一下你的现代污水管网系统吧！”

q.en: What main point does the book make about sewage handling through history?
q.zh: 关于历史上的污水处理，这本书的主要观点是什么？

options.en: People have always had perfect sewer systems.
options.zh: 人们一直拥有完美的排污系统。

options.en: Handling sewage has improved over time, from buckets and outhouses to modern treatment.
options.zh: 污水处理随着时间的推移不断改善，从倒桶、户外厕所发展到现代处理。

options.en: Nobody ever tried to deal with waste.
options.zh: 从来没有人尝试处理废弃物。

options.en: Sewage problems were solved completely in ancient Greece. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 污水问题在古希腊就被彻底解决了。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book contrasts ancient buckets thrown from windows and New York outhouses with today's modern treatment plants.
explain.zh: 书中将古代从窗口倒桶、纽约的户外厕所与今天的现代处理厂进行了对比。

prompt.en: Draw and label a diagram showing the route sewage takes after a toilet is flushed, then write a short paragraph explaining each step.
prompt.zh: 画一张带标注的示意图，展示冲马桶后污水的流动路线，然后写一小段文字解释每一步。

en: Start with the toilet tank and bowl, then show the sewer pipes joining into bigger pipes.
End your diagram at the treatment plant and mention the digester and drainage field as two ways waste is handled.
zh: 从马桶的水箱和便池开始，再画出污水管道汇集成更大的管道。
把示意图的终点画到处理厂，并提及消化池和排水场这两种处理废弃物的方式。
